title: Development of an immunodiagnostic assay for the detection of Sugarcane mosaic virus
author: Hafiz Muhammad Khalid
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: The following supporting information can be downloaded at: www.mdpi.com/xxx/s1,  Figure S1.  SnapGene map of the SCMVCpaj405-pET28 (a + ) expression construct for  E. coli .  Figure S2.  Transformation, ligation and restriction digestion analysis.  Figure S3.  6XHis-SCMVCpaj405 recombinantly expressed protein subcellular localization in E. coli BL21C+  Figure S4.  Optimization of indirect ELISA test for the detection of SCMV.  Figure S5.  RT-PCR-based confirmation of SCMV infection in sugarcane leaves. (a) Photograph of sugarcane leaves with and without symptoms (1= symptomatic; 2= non-symptomatic); (b) Total RNA extraction from sugarcane leaves (1= symptomatic; 2= non-symptomatic); (c) RT-PCR amplification of SCMVCpaj405 protein gene (1= symptomatic leaves, 2= healthy leaves), and M= 1kb DNA ladder.  Figure S6.  The graphical user interface for BepiPred-3.0 on Cp-SCMV protein.  Figure S7 . 3D model structures of SCMVCpaj405 protein constructed through I-TASSER  (a)  and SWISS-MODEL  (b) Figure S8.  Graphical representation of signal peptide for SCMVCpaj405 protein by Signal-5.0 prediction  Figure S9.  Multiple sequence alignment (amino acid) of the SCMV coat protein and SrMV coat protein. The red box indicates the common epitope with one amino acid difference at the 5th position between SCMV and SrMV.  Table S1  Predicted antigenic epitopes of SCMVCpaj405 fusion protein by GenScript “OptimumAntigen Design Tool.  Table S2  Predicted subcellular localization site of SCMVCpaj405 native and fusion proteins by SOSUI GramN .  Table S3.  Signal peptide score summary of SCMVCpaj405 predicted by SignalP-5.0 for Gram-negative bacteria.  Table S4  All predicted antigenic epitopes located in the SCMVCp, SrMVCp, and SCSMV.  Table S5  Common predicted antigenic epitopes located in the SCMVCp, SrMVCp, and SCSMVCp

keywords: sugarcane mosaic virus; polyclonal antibody; RT-PCR; immunodiagnostic; sugarcane
